title: Zombies Utils
description: A all-in-one mod for Hypixel Zombies. Targets speedrunners, features should match speedrum.com guidelines.
body:
# Summary

This mod is a client-sided QoL mod for Hypixel's Zombies minigame (in the Arcade Lobby).
Goal is to keep all features compliant to [speedrun.com](https://www.speedrun.com/hypixel_zombies). Note that this mod alone is not sufficient for these leaderboards' submissions (e.g. "keystrokes" are required and not planned for Zombies Utils).

# Features:
- timer:
  - accurate (server-synced game-time)
  - automated round splitting
  - tracking of personal bests (similar to LiveSplit)
    - simple management for categories
- SLA (display active window spawn-points)
- one hotkey for a chat message
- wave spawn timer
   - visually
   - auditory
   - Rocket Launcher offset
- player visibility toggle
- right-click CPS counter

![Example for the features: SLA HUD top left, timer and wave spawns bottom right, pb tracking notification in chat](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6d3bb0345957da3bb49c606f10e5f4fbfff498d6.png)

^^^ This refers to the 1.8.9 Forge edition.
Config GUI is implemented via Minecraft Forge's built-in mod config. For up-to-date details on configuration, hotkeys and commands check here: [1.8.9](https://git.stachel.xyz/zombies/zombies-utils_1.8.9/src/branch/master/README.md)

# Fabric Version
Fabric version is currently in development.
### features ported
- servertick-based timer (the best one so far perhaps)
- playervis
- wavetiming (auditory and visual), with mob prefixes
- tracking of personal bests (with categories)
- yaw / pitch HUD & entity counter (the latter is bugged in newer vanilla, but available in 1.8.9 debug HUD)
- chat macros
- 1.8.9 sneak height (essential in zombies)

I have plans to make fabric the primary version of this mod. It is certainly in a playable state, but not as complete as the legacy version.

Note that the fabric mod has a few required dependencies (Hypixel's official protocol extensions, my own Zombies library, YACL and Fabric API). You can find these linked on the version detail pages. Third-party launchers can take care of this for you.
